scenarios.name: help
scenarios.output:
Install and configure the $Product AI coding workflow

Usage:
  $PRODUCT [command]

Available Commands:
  completion  Generate the autocompletion script for the specified shell
  doctor      Check $Product setup without changing files
  help        Help about any command
  init        Initialize $Product's package-installed source checkout
  install     Install $Product-managed global workflow configuration
  pack        Discover and manage capability packs
  uninstall   Remove only $Product-managed artifacts
  update      Refresh $Product-managed tools and configuration

Flags:
  -h, --help      help for $PRODUCT
  -v, --version   version for $PRODUCT

Use "$PRODUCT [command] --help" for more information about a command.

Skill source: explicit override ($PRODUCT_SKILLS_SOURCE=$SOURCE)
$PRODUCT install dry-run: planned actions
- write-file: persist $Product state metadata ($HOME/.$PRODUCT/config.json)
- symlink: link managed skill ask-matt ($HOME/.agents/skills/ask-matt -> $SOURCE/engineering/ask-matt)
- symlink: link managed skill codebase-design ($HOME/.agents/skills/codebase-design -> $SOURCE/engineering/codebase-design)
- symlink: link managed skill grilling ($HOME/.agents/skills/grilling -> $SOURCE/productivity/grilling)
- symlink: link managed skill handoff ($HOME/.agents/skills/handoff -> $SOURCE/productivity/handoff)
- symlink: link managed skill loop-me ($HOME/.agents/skills/loop-me -> $SOURCE/in-progress/loop-me)
- symlink: link managed skill wayfinder ($HOME/.agents/skills/wayfinder -> $SOURCE/engineering/wayfinder)
- run: delegate Codex Engram setup through Homebrew binary ($BREW/bin/engram setup codex)
- run: delegate OpenCode Engram setup through Homebrew binary ($BREW/bin/engram setup opencode)
- write-codex-prompt: write Codex $Product prompt markers ($HOME/.codex/AGENTS.md)
- write-opencode-prompt: write OpenCode $Product prompt reference ($XDG/opencode/opencode.json -> $XDG/opencode/$PRODUCT.md)

## $Product global workflow

- Global skills live in ~/.agents/skills. When a task matches a skill, read that skill's SKILL.md before acting.
- Use ask-matt at ~/.agents/skills/ask-matt as the router when you are unsure which skill or workflow applies.
- Use Engram memory tools when available: search before past-work or project-sensitive tasks; save decisions, discoveries, bug fixes, and conventions; summarize sessions before finishing.
- Apply host delegation rules when this Codex session exposes subagent/delegation tools. If unavailable, proceed inline and mention that delegation was unavailable.
<!-- /$PRODUCT:skills-router --><!-- $PRODUCT:rules -->
## Dots Agent Rules

| Boundary | Rule |
| --- | --- |
| Always | Keep diffs surgical: every changed line must trace to the user request; mention unrelated issues instead of fixing them silently. |
| Always | Choose the simplest change that satisfies the request; avoid speculative abstractions, configurability, or features not explicitly needed. |
| Always | Plan before editing: think through the target behavior, inspect existing patterns, and state the smallest intended change before coding. |
| Always | Verify before declaring success: use focused checks while iterating, then run the repo-required checks when the task is complete. |
| Always | Use sandboxed HOME/config paths for dotfiles behavior; never validate by writing to the operator's real home config. |
| Ask first | Stop when the safe path is unclear, the scope would broaden, or an action could mutate real user configuration. |

## Delegation

For non-trivial work, load the delegation skill when available. Use it for Delegation Preflight, safe slice selection, skip reasons, and final reporting. Keep external project state in the main agent.
